description: An ordered map based on a B-Tree. Unlike HashMap, keys in a BTreeMap are always sorted. Use this when you need your keys in a consistent order. Python equivalent: dict (but always sorted by key, similar to sorted(dict.items())).

methods.name: new
methods.example:
use std::collections::BTreeMap;
let mut map: BTreeMap<String, i32> = BTreeMap::new();

methods.name: insert
methods.signature: fn insert(&mut self, key: K, value: V) -> Option<V>
methods.description: Inserts a key-value pair. If the key already existed, the old value is returned.

methods.name: get
methods.description: Returns a reference to the value for the given key, or None if the key is not present.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes a key from the map and returns its value if the key was present.

methods.name: contains_key
methods.description: Returns true if the map contains the specified key.

methods.name: iter
methods.description: Returns an iterator over key-value pairs, sorted by key.

methods.name: keys
methods.description: Returns an iterator over the keys, in sorted order. Python equivalent: dict.keys().

methods.name: values
methods.description: Returns an iterator over the values, ordered by their corresponding keys. Python equivalent: dict.values().

methods.name: len
methods.description: Returns the number of key-value pairs in the map.
